reason: This passage from Exodus 3-4 describes God's call to Moses at the burning bush and instructions to deliver the Israelites from Egyptian slavery. The text focuses on God's compassion for the people's suffering under Egyptian oppression (mentioning Egyptian taskmasters/נֹגְשָׁיו and Pharaoh as oppressors), and God commissioning Moses to liberate the people. There is no mention whatsoever of Hebrew leaders raging against the people. The passage emphasizes deliverance and liberation, not Hebrew leaders expressing anger toward their own people.
